name: Poolside Central Studio
subtitle: Balcony & Beach Walk
tagline: Perfectly located — the beach is your backyard
welcomeMessage: Welcome to the Poolside Central Studio! This chic, compact studio is perfectly positioned in the heart of Paphos, just steps away from the historic port. Everything you need is here — fresh linens, a fully equipped kitchen, a sunny balcony, and a shared pool to cool off in. Make yourself at home and enjoy every sun-soaked moment!

checkin.keyLocation: The key box is located on the left side of the wall as you face the main gate. For more details see photos 3 & 4 in the Getting Here section above.
steps: Enter the main building through the glass doors on Protaras Avenue
The studio is on the ground floor — door number 3B on the left
Use the key provided at reception to open the studio door
For after-hours arrival, use the key drop box (code: 4321) next to the lobby door
The pool access card is in the welcome envelope on the kitchen counter
checkin.notes: The shared pool is open 8am–9pm. Pool towels are NOT provided — please bring your own beach towels. The pool area has sunbeds for all guests.

gettingHere.byCar: Head to the Kings Avenue Mall in Paphos — the Byzantium Gardens complex is right next door. Use the Google Maps link above to navigate to the entrance. ⚠️ Google Maps navigation can be misleading for this address — for the last stretch it may route you through a road that is now a pedestrian promenade. Go around the block instead to reach the front entrance. See the step-by-step photo guide below for full directions.
gettingHere.parking: Once you have the keys, use the chip/proxy on the keychain to open the main gate. Parking is free — ideally park under Block C, but you can leave your car anywhere in the complex, even by the fence, as long as you're not blocking anything. To exit by car, pull up in front of the STOP / WAIT sign at the main gate — the doors open automatically.
gettingHere.publicTransport: From Paphos Airport, take Bus 612 directly and get off at the Kings Avenue Mall bus stop — just a 3-min walk from the apartment. From there, follow the Google Maps link above to navigate to the complex entrance. If you're unsure where to get off, just ask the bus driver. Missed the stop? No worries — the next stop is the Tombs of the Kings main station, just a 5-min walk to the apartment. ⚠️ Buses only accept cash — cards are not accepted. Fare is €2.50 per ride per person, so make sure to have coins or small notes on you.
gettingHere.taxi: City Cab Paphos (+357 26 933 333). Journey from Paphos Airport is approximately 15 minutes.

directionPhotos.caption: Navigate to the Google Maps location — the complex is 2 blocks to the left of Kings Avenue Mall. The red marks show roads to avoid; follow the correct route to reach the front entrance. You will also see a No Entry sign at the entrance — you can disregard it, it applies to non-residents only. As a guest you are allowed to drive in.
Once you reach the entrance, find the key boxes on the left-hand wall.
Use the small plastic chip (proxy) on the keychain — tap it on this reader to open the entrance door.
Open the left box of the 2nd row (highlighted in red) with the code: 9900 to collect your keys.
Arriving by car? Use this machine to open the main gate — you'll find it on your right-hand side as you face the gate.
Once inside, keep going straight and pass Block B — Block C (your apartment) is just ahead.
At Block C, take the entrance on the left and head to the first floor — there is a lift available.
Look for door No. 106, right next to the elevator.
Exiting by car: pull up in front of the STOP / WAIT sign at the main gate — the doors open automatically.

houseRules: No parties or events — quiet hours 11pm to 8am
Clean dishes immediately and keep the kitchen counter clear — Cyprus's warm weather can attract insects
Please use the bin next to the toilet — do not flush toilet paper (Cyprus plumbing cannot handle it)
Place the metallic key in the electricity plug by the door when inside; remove it every time you leave
Shared pool: no glass, no diving, be mindful of other guests
Close balcony door and windows when air conditioning is running
Return keys to the keybox and reset the code at checkout
Treat the apartment with care — please report any breakages promptly

appliances.name: Electricity
appliances.instructions: To have electricity, place the metallic key in the key plug on the wall next to the door, just like in hotels. Remember to remove it and take your keys with you every time you leave the apartment. Important: every socket in Cyprus has its own switch on the wall next to it. If an appliance is not working, check that the socket switch is turned on. You will also find EU adapters around the apartment for your convenience.

appliances.name: Induction Hob
appliances.instructions: Touch the ON button in the centre of the hob to activate. Tap the zone you want to use and press + to increase heat level. Use 3–4 for most cooking, 7–9 for boiling. Always use the induction-compatible pots and pans provided. Wipe the surface clean after each use with the cloth in the drawer.

appliances.name: Kitchen
appliances.instructions: The kitchen includes an electric stove, electric oven, a basic coffee maker and grinder. You're welcome to use all of them, please clean each appliance after use. Basic cooking ingredients are provided (salt, pepper, olive oil) and coffee beans, help yourself! Important: Cyprus's warm weather can attract insects. Please clean dishes promptly, dispose of food scraps immediately, and make sure nothing is left on the counter or dropped on the floor.

appliances.name: Washing Machine
appliances.instructions: You'll find the washing machine in the kitchen. The Quick Wash setting works well for most loads. If you have any questions, don't hesitate to reach out!

appliances.name: Dishwasher
appliances.instructions: The dishwasher is available for your convenience. Tabs are provided under the sink. We recommend using the Eco program: it's energy-efficient and works perfectly for everyday dishes.

appliances.name: Shower & Hot Water
appliances.instructions: It usually takes 2-3 minutes for the water to get warm, this is normal. If the water doesn't warm up, there may not have been enough sun for the solar panels that day. In that case, press the heating button next to the bathroom door and wait 20 minutes. Important: turn off the heating system before stepping into the shower. (This is only needed around 10% of the time. Cyprus is usually very sunny! ☀️)

appliances.name: Terraces & Umbrella
appliances.instructions: The apartment has two terraces. Feel free to set them up as you like. Outdoor cushions and décor are stored inside and can be arranged on the terraces. For the large patio umbrella: do not use it in windy conditions, close it at night or when not in use, and make sure it is securely closed and tied before checkout.

appliances.name: Pool Access
appliances.instructions: The pool key is on the key chain with your apartment keys. The shared pool is usually quiet. Please be mindful of other guests and observe any rules posted in the pool area. Use the beach towels provided in the apartment for the pool, not the bathroom towels.

appliances.name: Bathroom
appliances.instructions: Fresh towels, a hair dryer, and basic toiletries are provided. If you need anything extra, just let us know. Important: Cyprus's plumbing cannot handle toilet paper. Please use the bin next to the toilet instead. Thank you!

appliances.name: Drinking Water
appliances.instructions: Tap water in Cyprus is generally safe, but many guests prefer bottled water. Large bottles are available at any supermarket nearby.

appliances.name: Plants
appliances.instructions: You'll find a few plants around the apartment, they help keep the space feeling natural and cozy. If you're staying for more than a few days, we'd really appreciate a water every 3-4 days. Thank you! 🌿

items.name: Venus Beach
items.tip: Closest useful beach. 15-20 min on foot. Quick swim, sunset, convenience.

items.name: Faros Beach
items.tip: 25-35 min on foot or a short drive. Sunset, casual swim.

items.name: Sandy Beach, Chloraka
items.tip: Less touristy, local feel. Good for sunset. Better when the sea is calm.

items.name: Municipal Baths, Kato Paphos
items.tip: City swimming spot, not a sandy beach. Handy for a quick dip near the promenade.

items.name: Alykes Beach, Kato Paphos
items.tip: Organised beach with facilities, good for families. Easier by car or bus than on foot.

items.name: Coral Bay Beach
items.tip: The safest best beach choice near Paphos. Sandy, organised, easy. Hard to go wrong.

items.name: Laourou / Corallia Beach
items.tip: My preferred alternative to Coral Bay. Similar convenience, usually calmer.

items.name: Sea Caves, Peyia
items.tip: Dramatic coastline. Better for scenery and sunset photos than a classic beach day.

items.name: Agios Georgios Beach, Peyia
items.tip: Small beach, good for snorkelling. Combine with Sea Caves and Oniro by the Sea.

items.name: White River Beach / Toxeftra
items.tip: Raw and beautiful, wild coast. Better for exploring than sunbeds.

items.name: Lara Beach / Lara Bay
items.tip: One of the most special beaches in the Paphos area. Turtle nesting site. Rough access, no facilities.

items.name: Petra tou Romiou (Aphrodite's Rock)
items.tip: Legendary birthplace of Aphrodite. Beautiful scenery and photos, but rocky and not always ideal for swimming.

items.name: Latchi Harbour (Blue Lagoon boat trips)
items.tip: Best starting point for Blue Lagoon without off-road driving. Boat trips depart from here.

items.name: Blue Lagoon, Akamas
items.tip: Most impressive water in the Paphos area. Not a drive-up beach. Take a boat from Latchi.

items.name: Tombs of the Kings
items.tip: Closest and most important historical site. Rock-cut tombs from the Hellenistic and Roman periods, part of the UNESCO listed Paphos archaeological area.

items.name: Paphos Coastal Walkway
items.tip: Walk along the coastline toward Lighthouse Beach, the harbour, and Paphos Castle. Easy and scenic, especially at sunset.

items.name: Lighthouse Beach & Lighthouse
items.tip: Relaxed halfway point between Avlida and the harbour. Good for a scenic walk with a beach stop.

items.name: Old Town Paphos & Kennedy Square
items.tip: Good for cafes, local streets, small shops, and a more authentic Paphos feel. Best visited late afternoon.

items.name: Kato Paphos Archaeological Park
items.tip: Top cultural site in Cyprus. Roman mosaics considered among the finest in the eastern Mediterranean, UNESCO listed.

items.name: Paphos Harbour & Castle
items.tip: Easy evening plan. Byzantine fort rebuilt by the Lusignans, then Venetians, then Ottomans in the 16th century.

items.name: Agios Neophytos Monastery
items.tip: Beautiful half-day stop. Founded in the 12th century by Saint Neophytos in a secluded valley north of Paphos.

items.name: Sea Caves & Edro III Shipwreck
items.tip: Scenic coast, great for photos and sunset. Combine with Oniro by the Sea and Agios Georgios.

items.name: Agios Georgios Archaeological Site
items.tip: Quiet ruins with sea views, off the tourist trail. Good if already heading toward Sea Caves or Lara.

items.name: Petra tou Romiou (Aphrodite's Rock)
items.tip: Most iconic scenic stop in Cyprus. Better for views and mythology than swimming.

items.name: Akamas Peninsula
items.tip: Wild nature, viewpoints, walking trails. Road conditions vary. Best for adventurous guests.

items.name: Kings Avenue Mall
items.tip: Shopping, food court, cinema. Practical for an indoor break on hot or very windy days.

items.name: Paphos Harbour
items.tip: One of the easiest evening plans from Avlida. Dinner, drinks, and a relaxed walk by the sea.

items.name: Paphos Aphrodite Waterpark
items.tip: Best family attraction nearby. Easy half-day or full-day option on hot days.

items.name: Coral Bay & Corallia Beach
items.tip: Easiest classic beach excursion. Sand, sunbeds, and facilities. Hard to go wrong.

items.name: Horse Riding
items.tip: Nice alternative to beach and history. Book in advance, especially in high season.

items.name: Adonis Baths Waterfalls
items.tip: Popular excursion, road can be rough. Better for adventurous guests than polished sightseeing.

items.name: Sea Caves & Edro III Shipwreck
items.tip: Easy scenic excursion. Great for photos and sunset. Combine with Oniro by the Sea.

items.name: Paphos Zoo
items.tip: Good family option near Coral Bay. Easy to combine with Sea Caves or a beach day.

items.name: Avakas Gorge
items.tip: One of the best nature walks in the region. Wear proper shoes. Avoid after heavy rain or extreme heat.

items.name: Wine Villages around Kathikas
items.tip: Great for adults wanting a local experience. Better with a tour if everyone is tasting.

items.name: Akamas Jeep Safari
items.tip: Best for guests who want wild nature without driving difficult roads themselves. One of the best preserved areas in Cyprus.

items.name: Polis & Latchi
items.tip: Calmer, more local side of Cyprus. Good for lunch, a coastal walk, and Blue Lagoon boat departures.

items.name: Blue Lagoon Boat Trip from Latchi
items.tip: One of the best full-day excursions from Paphos. Many tours combine with Akamas and Baths of Aphrodite.

items.name: Baths of Aphrodite
items.tip: Small site, but beautiful surroundings. Best combined with Latchi, Blue Lagoon, and Polis.

items.name: Omodos Village
items.tip: One of the prettiest village excursions from Paphos. Works well as part of a Troodos or wine route day.

items.name: Kourion Archaeological Site
items.tip: Excellent day trip toward Limassol. Ancient theatre with sea views is the highlight.

items.name: Limassol Marina & Old Town
items.tip: More urban and polished than Paphos. Best combined with Kourion or Omodos.

items.name: Troodos Mountains & Villages
items.tip: Best full-day escape from the coast. Villages, monasteries, wine routes, and cooler air.

items.name: Paphos Coastal Walkway
items.tip: Best no-car option. Walk from the Tombs of the Kings area toward Lighthouse Beach, the harbour, and Paphos Castle.

items.name: Lighthouse Beach Coastal Walk
items.tip: Practical from Avlida. Good for guests who want nature without a proper hike.

items.name: Tombs of the Kings coastal loop
items.tip: Combines ancient tombs, open landscape, and coastline. Not a formal trail, but a very nice loop.

items.name: Sea Caves coastal walk, Peyia
items.tip: Easy scenic outing. Combine with Edro III Shipwreck and Oniro by the Sea.

items.name: Avakas Gorge Nature Trail
items.tip: Best nature trail near Paphos. Follows Avgas River through limestone formations. Wear proper shoes. Avoid after heavy rain.

items.name: Aphrodite's Rock coastal walk
items.tip: Scenic stop, not a long hike. Good when driving toward Limassol or the airport.

items.name: Lara Beach & Toxeftra coastal area
items.tip: Beautiful but rough access. Wild coast and turtle nesting areas. Respect the environment.

items.name: Smigies Nature Trail, Neo Chorio
items.tip: Less demanding than Aphrodite or Adonis. Short route 2.5 km (1hr), long route 6 km (2hrs).

items.name: Baths of Aphrodite short walk
items.tip: Gentle nature stop, not a real hike. Best combined with Latchi or a Blue Lagoon boat trip.

items.name: Aphrodite Nature Trail, Akamas
items.tip: One of the most beautiful hikes in the region. Wide views over Polis, Latchi, and Akamas.

items.name: Adonis Nature Trail, Akamas
items.tip: Views over Polis Chrysochous, Paphos Forest, and Akamas. Connects with Smigies and Aphrodite trails.

items.name: Troodos Nature Trails
items.tip: Best full-day hiking option in summer when the coast is too hot. Cooler forest trails and mountain scenery.

eatAndDrink.name: Karlina Restaurant
eatAndDrink.tip: Easiest no-car dinner option near Avlida. Ranked among the top restaurants near Tombs of the Kings.

eatAndDrink.name: Dias Zeus Greek Restaurant
eatAndDrink.tip: Reliable nearby option on Tombs of the Kings Avenue. Greek and Mediterranean with strong guest ratings.

eatAndDrink.name: Tea for Two
eatAndDrink.tip: Good for an easy breakfast or lunch close to home. Friendly service and great value.

eatAndDrink.name: Psarou Fish Tavern
eatAndDrink.tip: Cozy tavern known for fresh fish, traditional meze, and a friendly local vibe.

eatAndDrink.name: Kingfisher Taverna
eatAndDrink.tip: Small, relaxed taverna serving high-quality fish dishes and local favourites in a more upscale setting.

eatAndDrink.name: Three Little Pigs
eatAndDrink.tip: Local favourite with high-quality food, great service, and a lovely atmosphere. Their house wine is a treat!

eatAndDrink.name: Kings Avenue Mall Food Court
eatAndDrink.tip: Not special, but very practical. Good for families, quick food, coffee, or escaping the heat.

eatAndDrink.name: Old Town Paphos cafés
eatAndDrink.tip: More local feeling than the harbour. Nice for brunch, coffee, or early evening drinks.

eatAndDrink.name: Pallia Ilektriki
eatAndDrink.tip: A good Old Town option. Ranked among popular places near Old Town Paphos.

eatAndDrink.name: Ta Pinakia
eatAndDrink.tip: Good if you want something less harbour-focused. Well-rated near Old Town Paphos.

eatAndDrink.name: O Firis
eatAndDrink.tip: Modern twist on Cypriot cuisine with generous portions. Live music almost every evening. Book ahead!

eatAndDrink.name: Paphos Harbour restaurants
eatAndDrink.tip: Touristy but reliable. Many options for dinner, drinks, and a sunset walk by the sea.

eatAndDrink.name: King's Restaurant
eatAndDrink.tip: Popular harbour area choice. Good fish and Cypriot food.

eatAndDrink.name: Theo's Restaurant
eatAndDrink.tip: Good for seafood near the harbour. Sea bass, octopus, prawns, and calamari.

eatAndDrink.name: The Moorings Café Restaurant
eatAndDrink.tip: More about the harbour atmosphere than fine dining. Easy and relaxed.

eatAndDrink.name: West End Hotel & Restaurant
eatAndDrink.tip: Family-run with generous portions and delicious seafood, steps from the sea.

eatAndDrink.name: Oniro by the Sea
eatAndDrink.tip: One of the best scenic spots in the Paphos area. Combine with Sea Caves and Edro III shipwreck.

eatAndDrink.name: Kathikas wine village restaurants
eatAndDrink.tip: More authentic than the seafront. Best for a longer lunch or early dinner with wine tasting.

eatAndDrink.name: Latchi Harbour tavernas
eatAndDrink.tip: Best combined with a Blue Lagoon boat trip, Baths of Aphrodite, or a Polis day trip.

eatAndDrink.name: Lidl
eatAndDrink.tip: Budget-friendly with a wide range of groceries. Great for stocking up on basics.

eatAndDrink.name: AlphaMega
eatAndDrink.tip: Large supermarket with a wide variety of fresh produce and local Cypriot items.

shopping.name: Kings Avenue Mall
shopping.tip: The main shopping mall in Paphos. Wide range of shops, restaurants and a cinema.

shopping.name: Paphos Market
shopping.tip: Covered marketplace with stalls for clothing, bags, jewelry, beach gear and local souvenirs.

shopping.name: Old Town
shopping.tip: Wander through the beautiful Old Town and discover many small boutique stores and artisan shops.

gettingAround.name: Bus
gettingAround.info: The central bus station (Tombs of the Kings) is an 18-min walk (~1.3 km) away, with direct connections to main attractions, the airport, and nearby towns.

gettingAround.name: RideNow (Car Sharing)
gettingAround.info: Car sharing works well around Paphos, including to and from the airport. Download the app, sign up in 5 minutes. Cars typically available within a 5-10 min walk.

gettingAround.name: Bolt (Taxi)
gettingAround.info: Budget-friendly taxi app. Recommended for getting around Paphos without the uncertainty of metered cabs.

contacts.name: Emergency Services
contacts.note: Police, Ambulance, Fire. Free from any phone

contacts.name: Paphos District Hospital
contacts.note: Main hospital in Paphos, approx. 10 min drive

emergency.nearestHospital: Paphos District Hospital, approx. 10 min drive.
emergency.nearestPharmacy: Several pharmacies within walking distance on the main road. Ask us if you need a recommendation.

steps: Remove all items from the fridge and dispose of leftover food
Strip the bed and leave linens folded at the foot of the bed
Wash any dishes used and leave them drying on the rack
Empty the rubbish bin and take the bag to the wheelie bins in the car park
Close all windows and the balcony door
Switch off the air conditioning, all lights, and the TV
Return the room key and pool card to reception (or leave on the counter for late departure)
WhatsApp us to let us know you've left — thanks so much for staying!
checkout.lateCheckout: Late checkout is generally not available due to our tight housekeeping schedule. Please let us know if you have a special circumstance and we'll try our best.
